title: Wrong inner join result
description:
### What happens?

Dear DuckDB Developers,
Please see the following cases.
```sql
CREATE TABLE t0(c0 INT);
CREATE TABLE t1(c0 BOOL);

INSERT INTO t1(c0) VALUES (false);
INSERT INTO t0(c0) VALUES (NULL);

SELECT * FROM t0 INNER JOIN t1 on NOT(t1.c0) and ((false)<(((IFNULL(t0.c0, 1)))));
-- empty result
-- expected result: NULL, false

```
 Expected result: https://www.db-fiddle.com/f/e7u1hbDEh3pP5HburohfsC/0


### To Reproduce

```sql
CREATE TABLE t0(c0 INT);
CREATE TABLE t1(c0 BOOL);

INSERT INTO t1(c0) VALUES (false);
INSERT INTO t0(c0) VALUES (NULL);

SELECT * FROM t0 INNER JOIN t1 on NOT(t1.c0) and ((false)<(((IFNULL(t0.c0, 1)))));
-- empty result
-- expected result: NULL, false

```

### OS:

MacOS

### DuckDB Version:

DuckDB v1.3.0-dev3620 (Development Version) ee225e83f7

### DuckDB Client:

CLI

### Hardware:

_No response_

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t2(c0 INT, c1 BOOL);
INSERT INTO t2(c0, c1) VALUES (NULL, true);
CREATE TABLE t3(c0 FLOAT, c1 INT);
INSERT INTO t3(c0, c1) VALUES (1.5, NULL);
SELECT * FROM t2 INNER JOIN t3 ON (t2.c0 IS NULL OR t3.c1 < 2);
key_bug_features: INNER JOIN with complex condition on NULL values
